name: Wholesome Brisket with Roasted Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 4 3/4 lb to 5 lb brisket, trimmed but not completely
3 small onions (about 12 ounces total), peeled, root end kept intact, cut into half lengthwise, each half cut into 3 wedges
1 lb carrots, trimmed, peeled, and cut into 3- to 4-inch pieces
2 cups drained and mashed whole plum tomatoes plus 1/4 cup reserved roasting liquid or one 16-ounce can whole tomatoes with liquid
Freshly ground black pepper - to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Place one rack in the center of the oven and another in the bottom. Heat oven to 500 degrees F.
2. Place brisket, fat side down, in a 14 x 12 x 2-inch roasting pan. Do not use a shallow pan for this, or later the juices might run over and out.
3. Put the garlic in the pan. Drizzle with 1 teaspoon of the oil. Using your hands, rub oil onto garlic.
4. Put onion wedges and carrots into a smaller, 12 x 8 x 1 1/2-inch roasting pan. Drizzle with remaining oil. Using your hands, rub oil over all the pieces until they are well coated. Arrange onion wedges on their sides, so that they touch carrots as little as possible.
5. Roast vegetables on center rack of oven for 30 minutes. Turn with a spatula and roast 15 more minutes.
6. Roast the brisket simultaneously on the bottom rack of the oven for 15 minutes. Turn over so fat side is up. Roast 30 more minutes.
7. Remove both pans from oven. Lower heat to 275 degrees F, leaving the door ajar a minute or two to reduce temperature more quickly.
8. Leave the brisket and garlic in the large pan and add 1/4 cup of the water. If the water does not bubble up from the heat of the pan, put pan on stove over medium-high heat.
9. With a wooden spoon, scrape around the brisket to scoop up and remove brown bits from the bottom of pan.
10. Put onions and carrots into brisket pan. Place smaller pan on top of stove. Add remaining water and bring contents to a boil while scraping the bottom vigorously with a wooden spoon. Pour liquid into brisket pan.
11. Add roasted plum tomatoes and reserved roasting liquid or, if using canned tomatoes, stick a small knife into opened can of tomatoes and puree until only small pieces remain. Pour contents over vegetables and around brisket. Put bay leaf into liquid. Cover tightly with foil.
12. Roast on center rack of oven for 3 1/2 hours. Remove foil, increase temperature to 500 degrees F, and roast 15 more minutes or until brisket is nicely browned.
13. Remove from heat. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Using the back of a spoon, smash softened garlic out of each section into liquid. Discard empty papers. Transfer brisket to a platter and cover with foil tent. Use slotted spoon to transfer vegetables to a large serving bowl or plate. Discard bay leaf.
14. Pour remaining liquid into measuring cup. Let sit long enough for fat to rise to surface, about 15 minutes. Skim fat off surface.
15. To serve, cut brisket against grain into long thin strips. Arrange 2 to 3 slices on each plate. Spoon vegetables and moisten everything with generous amount of liquid.
